title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Andean indigenous origin
description: Cusco Peru — Erythroxylum coca cultivated and chewed by Indigenous Quechua + Aymara peoples for 8,000+ years. Sacred plant (Mama Coca).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Andean indigenous origin
description: Cochabamba Bolivia — traditional coca heartland (Yungas + Chapare regions). Chewing is legal and cultural foundation.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Spanish colonial exploitation
description: Cusco — Spanish colonial authorities initially tried to ban coca, then reversed when they realized it enabled Indigenous silver miners to work longer hours at Potosí mines.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Spanish colonial exploitation
description: Potosí silver mines — coca chewing fuels massive colonial silver extraction. Estimated 8 million Indigenous deaths linked to Potosí.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: 19th-century pharmaceutical isolation
description: Bern / Zurich — German chemist Albert Niemann isolates cocaine alkaloid 1860.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: 19th-century pharmaceutical isolation
description: Vienna — Sigmund Freud publishes 'Über Coca' 1884 praising cocaine; Karl Koller pioneers cocaine surgical anaesthesia.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: 19th-century pharmaceutical isolation
description: Atlanta 1886 — John Pemberton combines coca + kola nut to create Coca-Cola (cocaine removed 1903).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Drug War + Plan Colombia
description: Washington DC — 1970 Controlled Substances Act Schedule II. 1999 Plan Colombia $10B+ aerial fumigation program.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Drug War + Plan Colombia
description: Bogotá Colombia — fumigation campaigns, military operations, FARC conflict.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: US Drug War + Plan Colombia
description: La Paz Bolivia — 2005 Evo Morales elected, former coca growers' union leader. Makes coca chewing legal globally via UN reservation.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Quechua + Aymara names
description: Quechua — 'kuka'. Ancestral name; cultural + sacred meaning.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Quechua + Aymara names
LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Quechua + Aymara names
description: Spanish — 'coca'. Preserved Quechua word; 'cocaína' is the isolated alkaloid.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Quechua + Aymara names
description: English — 'coca' + 'cocaine'. 'Coca-Cola' still carries the Quechua root.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Colombia — world's largest cocaine supplier, despite Plan Colombia. Catatumbo + Nariño + Cauca + Putumayo main regions.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Peru — VRAEM + Huallaga valleys. Second-largest producer.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Bolivia — legal coca (chewing + tea) + illicit cocaine. Third producer.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Miami — main US entry point for Colombian cocaine via Caribbean + Central American routes.
